rationale: Spiller et al. (2013) is used in the main empirical testing rather than as general background. In Study 4, the authors explicitly run a floodlight analysis and report Johnson-Neyman cutoff points to interpret the interaction between mortality salience and chronic materialism. The paper also presents a figure tied to this analysis, showing that the cited method is functionally important to hypothesis evaluation.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Spiller et al. (2013)'s floodlight (Johnson-Neyman) probing approach to analyze a moderation effect in Study 4. It uses the method to identify ranges of materialism where mortality salience significantly increases or decreases scope sensitivity.
